reasoning: On September 10, 2025, Larry Ellison surpassed Elon Musk intraday due to a massive surge in Oracle stock. However, multiple sources note Musk regained the top spot by the close of the trading day. Since the Bloomberg Billionaires Index officially updates at the end of the trading day, it is highly contested whether this brief intraday flip satisfies the 'at any point' clause in the resolution criteria, as reflected by the market bouncing to 90% before settling lower (around 66%). Because the Polymarket has not been definitively resolved by November 2025, either there is a pending dispute resolution or the Sept 10 event was officially ruled insufficient (meaning a future drop would be required, which is unlikely given Musk's massive wealth margin). Balancing the strong argument for a YES resolution based on the 'at any point' literal text against the possibility that it didn't register on Bloomberg's official end-of-day daily publish, I forecast a 72% chance of resolving YES.
batch_queries: Elon no longer world's richest before 2026?
